target.title: Fix Mattermost open DM validation
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a generic, channel-agnostic `config validate` warning when a channel's effective `dmPolicy`/`allowFrom` would silently drop every DM (`dmPolicy:"open"` without `"*"`, or `dmPolicy:"allowlist"` with an empty allowlist). Not Mattermost-specific.
- Surface it wherever config is checked: `openclaw config validate` (text + `--json`) and on config load (`logger.warn`).
- Keep the Mattermost monitor DM-drop log hint that explains a dropped DM at runtime.

## Behavior: warn, not reject
The config stays valid; we warn instead of rejecting. This matches `security audit`/`doctor`, preserves runtime semantics (open policy + restrictive allowlist is a legal runtime state), and avoids breaking existing-but-usable configs on upgrade.

## Root cause
`config validate` validates channels through generated JSON Schema, which drops the per-channel Zod `superRefine` dependency checks (`requireOpenAllowFrom` / `requireAllowlistAllowFrom`). This gap affects every channel that uses the shared top-level `dmPolicy`/`allowFrom` contract, not just Mattermost. The rule is now centralized in a single evaluator (`evaluateDmPolicyAllowFromDependency` in `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`) shared by the Zod refinements and the CLI validator, so it cannot drift between surfaces.

## Effective DM resolution (no false positives)
Warnings resolve policy/allowFrom through the shared `resolveChannelDm*` helpers using each channel's `dmAllowFromMode`, so they honor the legacy `dm.*` aliases and account -> channel inheritance the runtime uses. Channels with `dmAllowFromMode: "nestedOnly"` (canonical fields under `dm.*`) are skipped because their shape does not match this warning's top-level paths.

## Operator-facing note
`openclaw config validate --json` now includes a `warnings` array on the valid-config path (additive field). This is deliberate so tooling can surface non-fatal config problems now that this check warns rather than rejects.

## Status scope
The earlier Mattermost-only channel-status surface (and its `status-issues` module) was removed in favor of the generic `config validate` + config-load warnings and the runtime drop-log hint.

## Tests
- `pnpm test src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts` — generic (telegram), Mattermost channel + account scope, Discord legacy `dm.allowFrom` suppression, wildcard suppression, account-inherits-wildcard
- `pnpm test src/cli/config-cli.test.ts` — prints warnings while still reporting a valid config (text + JSON)
- `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-auth.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files>` and oxlint on changed files
- `git diff --check`
target.author: amknight
